message:
Simplify messaging tool implementation and update parity test

- messaging.rs: simplify send_message tool by removing redundant
  validation paths (-22 lines net)
- tool_description_parity.rs: conformance test adjustment for
  messaging tool changes
- .beads/issues.jsonl: issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -637,7 +637,7 @@
 {"id":"br-28mgh.7.4","title":"T7.4: Check database format and health","description":"Verify the database is in the correct format and healthy.

Checks:
1. Database file exists and is readable
2. Timestamp format is i64 (not TEXT) -- uses T3.1 detection
3. Schema version is current (all migrations applied)
4. FTS5 virtual tables exist and are functional
5. Integrity check: PRAGMA integrity_check
6. WAL mode is enabled
7. Row counts per table (sanity check)
8. Last message timestamp (is the data recent or stale?)

Status output:
  [PASS] Database: ~/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo/storage.sqlite3 (2.1 MB)
  [PASS] Timestamp format: i64 microseconds (Rust native)
  [PASS] Schema version: v3 (current)
  [PASS] Integrity check: ok
  [WARN] Database has Python TEXT timestamps - run `am migrate` to convert
  [FAIL] Database not found at expected path

 {"id":"br-28mgh.7.5","title":"T7.5: Doctor --fix automatic remediation","description":"Add --fix flag to `am doctor` that attempts automatic remediation.

For each fixable issue:
- Python alias found -> Comment it out (reuse T1.4 logic)
- PATH missing -> Append to shell rc (reuse T2.1 logic)
- MCP config points to Python -> Update to Rust (reuse T4.2 logic)
- Database needs migration -> Run migration (reuse T3.3 logic)
- Bearer token mismatch -> Sync tokens across configs

Each fix:
1. Print what will be changed
2. In interactive mode, prompt for confirmation
3. Apply the fix
4. Re-run the check to verify
5. Report success/failure

CLI:
  am doctor          # Check only, print issues
  am doctor --fix    # Attempt automatic remediation
  am doctor --json   # Machine-readable output

Exit codes:
  0: All checks pass (or all fixes succeeded)
  1: Issues found (check mode) or fixes failed
  2: Some checks could not be run (missing permissions, etc.)

-{"id":"br-28mgh.8","title":"T8: Testing and Validation","description":"Track 8: Testing and Validation

End-to-end tests that verify the entire install-migrate-run pipeline works correctly
across platforms. This includes both automated tests and a manual test checklist.

## Scope
- E2E test: fresh install on clean system (no Python)
- E2E test: install over existing Python installation
- E2E test: database migration (real Python database)
- E2E test: MCP config takeover
- E2E test: self-update from v0.1.0 to v0.1.1
- E2E test: uninstall and rollback
- E2E test: doctor command detects all issues
- Docker-based CI test matrix (ubuntu, fedora, macOS-like)
- Manual test checklist for release validation
- Integration test for shell alias detection across bash/zsh/fish

## Key Constraint
Tests must be idempotent and leave no side effects (use tmpdir/Docker).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.941206491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T07:02:55.773580512Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.8","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1090,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.8","author":"DustyCardinal","text":"Added 11 integration tests for am doctor fix under br-28mgh.8: 2 clap parsing tests (defaults, all flags), 5 unit tests (fix_legacy_alias_line, fix_path_order, fix_mcp_config_entry, fixable_checks constant), 2 schema/help tests (json_doctor_fix_schema_stable, help_doctor_fix_lists_flags).  {"id":"br-28mgh.8.1","title":"T8.1: E2E test - fresh install on clean system","description":"E2E test that verifies fresh installation on a system with no prior mcp-agent-mail.

Test in Docker container (ubuntu:22.04):
1. Start with clean system (no Python, no Rust am)
2. Run: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash
3. Verify: am --version returns Rust version
4. Verify: mcp-agent-mail --version returns Rust version
5. Verify: am is a binary, not an alias
6. Verify: PATH includes ~/.local/bin
7. Verify: am doctor reports all green
8. Verify: am serve-stdio responds to MCP handshake

Also test macOS-like environment (homebrew paths, zsh as default shell).

 {"id":"br-28mgh.8.2","title":"T8.2: E2E test - install over Python","description":"E2E test that verifies installation over an existing Python mcp-agent-mail.

Test in Docker:
1. Set up Python mcp-agent-mail (clone, venv, alias in .zshrc)
2. Create test data: project, agent, messages, reservations
3. Run Rust installer: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash
4. Verify: am --version returns Rust version (NOT Python)
5. Verify: Python alias is commented out in .zshrc
6. Verify: Database migrated (timestamps are i64)
7. Verify: All test data accessible via Rust CLI
8. Verify: MCP configs updated to Rust binary
9. Verify: am doctor reports all green
10. Verify: Original database backed up

This is THE critical test -- it validates the entire migration pipeline end-to-end.

Remaining failures are product-behavior gaps (timestamp conversion, migrated data visibility, token carry-through, doctor JSON validity, backup artifact creation), not harness/setup crashes.","created_at":"2026-03-02T05:31:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.8.3","title":"T8.3: Unit tests for timestamp conversion","description":"Comprehensive unit tests for the timestamp conversion functions.

Test vectors (at minimum):
1. "2026-02-24 15:30:00.123456" -> known i64 value
2. "2026-02-24T15:30:00.123456" -> same value (T separator)
3. "2026-02-24 15:30:00" -> known i64 (no fractional)
4. "2026-02-24" -> midnight of that date
5. "1970-01-01 00:00:00" -> 0
6. "1969-12-31 23:59:59" -> negative value (pre-epoch)
7. NULL -> NULL preserved
8. "" -> treated as NULL
9. "not-a-date" -> error, logged and skipped
10. "2026-02-24 15:30:00.123456+00:00" -> strip tz, treat as UTC
11. Round-trip: convert Python->Rust->ISO string, verify matches original

Also test the full migration path:
12. Create Python-format database in test fixture
13. Run migration
14. Verify all rows converted correctly
15. Run migration again -> idempotent, no changes
16. Verify FTS5 still works after migration

 {"id":"br-2bbt.5.2","title":"Create ActionMenu for contextual per-item actions","description":"Create ActionMenu overlay for contextual actions on selected items:

1. ActionMenu struct: entries (Vec<ActionEntry>), selected_index, anchor_position
2. ActionEntry: label, description, keybinding (Option<String>), action closure, is_destructive flag

Per-screen action sets:
- Messages: View body | Acknowledge | Mark read | Jump to thread | Jump to sender
- Reservations: Renew | Release | Force-release (-> modal) | View holder agent
- Agents: View profile | View inbox | View reservations | Send message
- Threads: View messages | Summarize | Search in thread
- Timeline: View details | Filter by type | Filter by source | Copy event text
- Contacts: View agent | Approve | Deny | Block

Trigger key: . (period) on selected item opens ActionMenu as floating overlay near item.
Rationale: Enter is reserved for primary action (expand/navigate/select). Period is the standard action-menu trigger in tools like GitHub CLI and vim. This avoids collision with deep-linking (Enter) and text input (Enter in compose/search).

Behavior:
- . on selected item opens ActionMenu anchored near the selected row
- Arrow keys navigate menu entries
- Enter executes selected action
- Esc closes menu without action
- Typing first letter of action label jumps to it (e.g., a -> Acknowledge)
- Menu shows keybinding shortcuts next to each entry
- Destructive actions marked with red text and trigger confirmation modal

## Tests
- Unit: per-screen action set correctness (each screen returns correct actions)
- Unit: first-letter jump navigation (typing a selects Acknowledge)
- Unit: is_destructive flag correctly set on force-release, block, clear actions
- Snapshot: action menu rendering anchored near row 5 in Large terminal
- Snapshot: action menu with 6 entries showing keybinding hints
- E2E: select message, press ., verify menu appears with correct actions, select Acknowledge, verify ack executed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.592124628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:10:23.865177548Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:10:23.865159384Z","close_reason":"ActionMenu module created (tui_action_menu.rs ~740 lines), integrated into MailAppModel with focus trapping and z-ordering. 7 unit tests pass. Per-screen action builders implemented for all screens.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modals","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.5.3","title":"Wire modals into destructive actions across all screens","description":"Connect ModalManager to all destructive actions in the TUI:

1. Reservations screen:
   - Force-release (current: direct keypress) -> now shows confirmation modal
   - Modal shows: reservation path, holder agent, TTL remaining
   - On confirm: execute force-release, show success toast

2. Explorer/Messages:
   - Clear all filters (if any clear-all action exists) -> confirmation modal
   
3. SystemHealth:
   - Any reset/restart action -> confirmation modal

4. Integration with ActionMenu:
   - When action is destructive, ActionMenu entry triggers modal instead of direct execution
   - Action entries marked with is_destructive flag

5. Ensure all modals emit toast on completion (success or cancellation logged)

## Tests
- E2E: force-release via action menu -> modal appears -> confirm -> reservation released -> toast shown
- E2E: force-release via action menu -> modal appears -> Esc -> reservation unchanged
- Unit: is_destructive flag correctly set on force-release, clear, reset actions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.768148321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:23:47.461580993Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:23:47.461563050Z","close_reason":"Action menu and modal wiring complete.  {"id":"br-2bbt.6","title":"[track] Animated State Transitions","description":"## Rationale (expanded with webapp inspiration)

The webapp uses CSS animations extensively: Urgent importance badges PULSE with animation (0.75s infinite), staggered card entrance animations on Projects page, hover effects with smooth transitions (300ms ease), activity feed items animate in with stagger delays, network graph nodes have physics-based movement.

Static displays in the TUI feel dead by comparison. frankentui provides: ftui_extras text_effects (color gradients, reveal modes, cursor styles), animation-aware rendering with tick-driven state, accessibility support via AM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION.

## Specific Animations to Implement

1. Urgent badge pulse (from webapp): Messages with importance=urgent get pulsing red badge. Cycle: bright red -> dim red -> bright red (1Hz). Appears on Dashboard, Messages list, Thread cards.

2. New message flash (from webapp activity feed): When a new message arrives, briefly highlight the row. Flash duration: 300ms (inverted colors, then fade to normal).

3. Reservation TTL color gradient (from webapp): Smooth transition green (>60%) -> yellow (30-60%) -> orange (10-30%) -> red (<10%). Updates every tick, not just at threshold crossings.

4. Agent status transition (from webapp hover effects): When agent becomes idle/inactive, fade color over 5 ticks. Active: bright, Idle: dimmed, Inactive: very dim.

5. Toast entrance/exit (from webapp): Entrance: slide in from right (4 ticks). Exit: fade out over 3 ticks.

6. Staggered list load (from webapp card entrance): New content appears with cascade effect (1 tick per row, max 10 ticks).

## Implementation
All animations are tick-driven (no separate threads). AnimState { phase: f32, active: bool } per widget. AM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION=true skips all (instant final state). O(1) per tick per widget.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 4 of 6 animations implemented
- All respect reduced-motion setting
- No performance impact
- Unit tests for animation state machine
- Snapshot tests with fixed tick count","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:35.338061376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:40:46.983180117Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:40:46.983157254Z","close_reason":"Completed: implemented 5/6 animation items with reduced-motion support and deterministic tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["animation","polish","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.6","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.6","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":367,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.6","author":"WindyLynx","text":"Implemented animation #4 (Agent status transition) in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/agents.rs`:

-{"id":"br-2bbt.7","title":"[track] JSON Tree View for Structured Data","description":"When message bodies or tool responses contain JSON, render with frankentui collapsible JSON tree view instead of plain text.

1. Auto-detect JSON in message body_md (try serde_json::from_str, if valid -> tree view)
2. Toggle between markdown view and JSON tree view with keybinding: J (capital J)
   - IMPORTANT: lowercase j is already used for navigation (help overlay scroll down, Search screen next-result). Capital J avoids all conflicts since it's only meaningful on detail panes where text display is active.
3. JSON tree supports: collapse/expand nodes, copy path, copy value
4. Syntax coloring: keys=cyan, strings=green, numbers=yellow, booleans=magenta, null=dim

Use ftui_extras JSON view widget if available, or compose from Tree widget + styling.

## Where JSON Toggle Applies
- Messages screen: detail pane when viewing message body
- Search screen: preview pane when viewing result
- Thread conversation view: expanded card body (br-2bbt.19)
- NOT on any screen where j/J is used for navigation

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON bodies render as collapsible tree
- Non-JSON bodies render as markdown (unchanged)
- Toggle between views with J (capital J only, NOT lowercase j)
- Collapse/expand works with Enter on nodes
- Snapshot tests for nested JSON rendering
- Unit test: auto-detection of valid/invalid JSON","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageOrchid","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:35.511024527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T06:55:22.521230058Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["json","tui","widgets"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.7","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reservation conflict blocked immediate execution: YellowPeak currently holds exclusive reservations on messages/search/threads screen files required for this bead.  {"id":"br-2bbt.8","title":"[track] Cross-Screen Deep Linking","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp has clickable links everywhere: agent names link to agent profiles, thread IDs link to thread views, message IDs link to message detail, project slugs link to project overview. The webapp breadcrumb navigation provides back-navigation context.

The TUI should mirror this: navigating between related entities should be fluid and intuitive.

## Implementation

1. DeepLink enum:
   - Agent(project_slug, agent_name) -> Agents screen, agent selected
   - Thread(project_slug, thread_id) -> Threads screen, thread expanded
   - Message(project_slug, message_id) -> Messages screen, message selected + detail open
   - Project(project_slug) -> Projects screen, project selected
   - Reservation(project_slug, reservation_id) -> Reservations screen, reservation selected
   - Search(query) -> Search screen with query pre-filled

2. Navigation stack (breadcrumb-like):
   - Push current screen + scroll position + selection state on navigate
   - Alt+Left pops stack (goes back to previous screen with state restored)
   - Backspace is NOT used (conflicts with text input in search bars, compose panel, etc.)
   - Stack depth limited to 10 entries (circular buffer, oldest dropped)
   - Stack shown in status bar breadcrumb: Dashboard > Messages > Thread #42

3. Deep linking mechanism (TUI-appropriate):
   - Enter on a selected ROW performs the screen's primary action:
     - Messages: expand message detail pane
     - Agents: view agent profile detail
     - Threads: expand thread conversation view
     - Timeline: expand event details
   - Deep linking to REFERENCED entities (e.g., sender name within a message) happens through:
     a. Action Menu (. key): includes "Jump to sender", "Jump to thread" entries (per br-2bbt.5.2)
     b. Command Palette (Ctrl+P): entity search finds agents/threads/messages by name
   - NOTE: TUI tables select whole rows, not individual cells. There is no "click on agent name within a message row" interaction like in the webapp. The Action Menu and Command Palette substitute for this.

4. Visual indicators for linkable contexts:
   - When detail pane shows a message, referenced entities (sender name, thread ID) are rendered in a distinct color (link_color semantic) to hint they're navigable
   - Status bar breadcrumb shows navigation path for context

5. Integration with Command Palette (br-2bbt.1):
   - Palette entity search results create DeepLinks
   - Palette shows recent navigation history as entries

6. Integration with Action Menu (br-2bbt.5):
   - Action menu entries include Jump to sender, Jump to thread, Jump to project
   - These create DeepLinks that push to navigation stack

## Tests
- Unit: DeepLink dispatch for all 6 link types
- Unit: navigation stack push/pop/overflow (>10 entries wraps)
- Unit: state restoration on back (scroll position, selection)
- Snapshot: status bar breadcrumb rendering at different depths
- E2E: navigate Messages -> select message -> press . -> Jump to sender -> verify Agents screen with sender selected -> Alt+Left -> verify back on Messages with original selection","notes":"Status hygiene closure: cross-screen deep linking is implemented across TUI screens via DeepLinkTarget + MailScreenMsg::DeepLink in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/mod.rs and routed by MailAppModel in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_app.rs. Action menu jump entries (Jump to sender/thread/project) are present in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_action_menu.rs, and many screens implement receive_deep_link handlers with tests (messages, threads, search, agents, reservations, projects, contacts, explorer, analytics, timeline).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:35.689520802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:00:47.733359803Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T04:00:47.733337100Z","close_reason":"Implemented (cross-screen deep linking) with broad in-tree coverage evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["navigation","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.8","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.8","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.9","title":"[track] Saved Filter Presets","description":"Save and recall complex filter/sort/group configurations:

1. On any screen with filters, Ctrl+S opens Save Preset dialog (name, optional description)
2. Presets stored in tui_persist state file
3. Ctrl+L opens Load Preset list (shows saved presets for current screen)
4. Delete preset with Del key in Load list
5. Presets are per-screen (Messages presets dont show on Timeline)

TERMINAL NOTE: Ctrl+S traditionally triggers XOFF flow control (freeze output) and
Ctrl+L traditionally clears screen in shell contexts. However, the TUI runs in raw
terminal mode where XOFF/XON is disabled and all key events are captured by the
application. frankentui handles this terminal setup, so Ctrl+S and Ctrl+L are safe
to use as application keybindings within the TUI.

Common preset examples:
- Messages: Unacked high-priority from last 24h
- Timeline: Errors and warnings only
- Reservations: Expiring within 10 minutes
- Explorer: All from specific agent

## Acceptance Criteria
- Save/Load/Delete preset lifecycle works
- Presets persist across TUI restarts
- Presets are screen-specific
- Ctrl+S and Ctrl+L work in raw terminal mode (XOFF disabled)
- Unit tests for preset serialization/deserialization
- Unit tests for save/load/delete lifecycle
- Unit tests for screen-scoped preset isolation","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"2026-02-19 LilacBirch: completed message-screen saved preset lifecycle in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/messages.rs after acquiring reservation. Added persisted per-screen preset storage namespace messages with Ctrl+S save dialog, Ctrl+L load dialog, Delete removal, snapshot/apply for query text + preset_index + inbox mode/local project, and help/context text updates. Implemented missing PTY integration coverage for message-screen preset lifecycle + screen-scope isolation.

Code changes:
1. Added test `messages_preset_shortcuts_persist_reload_and_preserve_other_screen_presets` in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/tests/pty_e2e_search.rs`.
   - Seeds a timeline preset namespace.
   - Exercises Messages Ctrl+S/Ctrl+L/Delete lifecycle.
   - Round-trip verifies saved preset values after local mutation.
   - Asserts deleting message presets does not remove timeline presets.
2. Added E2E suite case `case07c_messages_preset_lifecycle` in `tests/e2e/test_tui_v3_rendering.sh`.
3. Updated rendering-suite coverage comments to include the new preset case.

Validation:
- `rustfmt crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/tests/pty_e2e_search.rs` passed.
- `bash -n tests/e2e/test_tui_v3_rendering.sh` passed.
- RCH cargo test execution is currently blocked by upstream `/dp/asupersync` API skew across workers:
  - `http_client.rs` connect arity mismatch (missing `&Cx` arg)
  - missing `cx.now()` in `tls/{acceptor,connector}.rs`
